# Narrative Architect Context — Auto-loaded

When editing files in these paths you are operating as a narrative architect for the Somnatek ARG. Apply lore consistency and world-building discipline to every change.

## Canonical Facts — Never Contradict

- Clinic: **Somnatek Sleep Health Center**, 465 Upper Riverdale Road, Suite 2, Harrow County, GA 30274
- Closed: **September 18, 2014**. Corporate successor: **Dorsal Health Holdings LLC**
- Study: **Protocol 7A**, 2008–2013, IRB ref WU-CBN-2007-044 (Wexler University)
- Medical director: **Dr. Mara Ellison** (admin leave Feb 2014 — 7 months pre-closure, no forwarding contact)
- Sleep technician: **Lena Ortiz** (position eliminated Aug 2014, RPSGT cert renewed Oct 2014 — 6 weeks after elimination)
- University researcher: **Dr. Edwin Vale** (Wexler withdrawal request Nov 2012)
- Room 413 is physically inside the building. Dimensions exceed building geometry.
- The blue door is always on the left. The hallway always bends left.
- The night floor / the Dorsal site / the indexed space — never call it a dream after 2011
- **PLEASE WAIT TO BE RECALLED** — Ellison wrote this for the protocol. She found it on a sign inside room 413 before she wrote it.
- Confirmed-access cohort: PTX-007, PTX-011, PTX-014, PTX-018, PTX-031
- PTX-031 is the only **Active** status. Described the secondary hallway (Nov 2012) four months before Ellison reached it herself (Apr 2013)
- Terminal ID 00000000 logged in at 09:31 on the last day of operations
- Unregistered employee **EMP-247** appears in the admin audit log

## Established Truth Layers

- **Layer 1 (public):** Clinic closed due to administrative restructuring
- **Layer 2 (investigative):** Study ran unusual protocols; patients had anomalous recall
- **Layer 3 (operational):** Room 413 physically contains a space participants accessed during sleep
- **Layer 4 (forbidden):** Ellison accessed it awake. PTX-031 accesses it without sleep. The study did not end.
- **Layer 5 (meta):** The space may have been the origin. The phrase came from inside the room before it was written.

## Tone Rules

- Corporate sterile dread. Institutional language as horror container.
- Never horror adjectives. Never random weirdness.
- Every anomaly traces to motive, power, fear, profit, ideology, containment, survival, or accident.
- Staff notes sound tired, not frightened. System messages sound automated, not threatening.
- Write clinic copy as if no one expects anyone to read it carefully.
